translator: Swami Adidevananda (per Sri Ramakrishna Math edition; bhagavad-gita.us reproduces this English rendering of Ramanuja's Gita-bhasya)
verbatim_excerpt: 2.1 – 2.3 Sanjaya said — Lord said — When Arjuna thus sat, the Lord, opposing his action, said: ‘What is the reason for your misplaced grief? Arise for battle, abandoning this grief, which has arisen in a critical situation, which can come only in men of wrong understanding, which is an obstacle for
copyright_holder: Sri Ramakrishna Math, Chennai (publisher of Swami Adidevananda's English translation of Ramanuja's Gita-bhasya). Site bhagavad-gita.us reproduces the translation; we cite a short fair-use excerpt only.
notes: Full commentary length 414 chars. Ramanuja gives a single combined gloss for verses 2.1-2.3; the captured excerpt is a literal fair-use prefix of that combined commentary and applies to every verse in the cluster. Source uses non-IAST transliteration (e.g. 'Krsna', 'Bhisma', 'Drona') and curly quotes; preserved verbatim.
